meta.source: Official Nordic admissions portals + hand-curated Norwegian data

addiction-counsellor.specialisationNote: Addiction counsellors support people through substance recovery. A bachelor in sosialt arbeid or vernepleie, often with additional courses in rusproblematikk (substance issues).

adoption-specialist.specialisationNote: Adoption specialists handle adoption cases through Bufdir or accredited agencies. A bachelor in sosialt arbeid or barnevernspedagogikk plus several years in child welfare.

aerospace-engineer.specialisationNote: Aerospace Engineers share skills with Mechanical Engineers, particularly in designing systems and solving technical problems related to structures and materials.

ai-engineer.specialisationNote: AI engineers integrate machine-learning models into production systems. A bachelor in informatikk/computer science is the standard route; many add a master in Artificial Intelligence or Machine Learning. NTNU and UiO both offer AI specialisations.

ai-network-engineer.specialisationNote: AI Infrastructure Network Engineers require software development skills to design and manage networks for AI systems, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

ai-product-manager.specialisationNote: AI Product Managers often work closely with software developers to create AI-powered products, requiring a strong understanding of software development principles.

ai-researcher.specialisationNote: AI Researchers often work with software development, particularly in creating algorithms and applications for artificial intelligence.

ai-safety-researcher.specialisationNote: AI Safety & Ethics Researchers often have a background in software development, focusing on the ethical implications of AI technologies.

air-force-technician.specialisationNote: Air Force Technicians require mechanical skills similar to those of Mechanical Engineers, focusing on the maintenance and repair of complex systems.

air-traffic-controller.specialisationNote: Air Traffic Controllers work closely with Airline Pilots to ensure safe flight operations, making their training and skills relevant to aviation.

aircraft-maintenance-engineer.specialisationNote: Aircraft Maintenance Engineers share skills in mechanical systems and maintenance, making Mechanical Engineering a relevant study path in Norway.

anesthesiologist.specialisationNote: After the 6-year medical degree, complete LIS1 (18 months) then LIS2/3 anaesthesiology specialisation (5 years).

animator.specialisationNote: Animators create motion graphics and character animation for film, games, and ads. A bachelor in animation or visual communication. Volda and Westerdals are the strongest Norwegian programmes; KADK offers an animation route in Denmark.

app-developer.specialisationNote: An App Developer is a type of Software Developer who focuses specifically on creating mobile applications, sharing many skills and educational paths.

application-security-lead.specialisationNote: Application security leads guard the codebase against vulnerabilities. A bachelor in informatikk plus several years of security engineering experience. Senior individual-contributor or lead role.

applied-ai-engineer.specialisationNote: Applied AI engineers ship ML models into real products. A bachelor in informatikk plus practical experience with PyTorch/TensorFlow. The role overlaps heavily with software development — a CS degree is the strongest entry.

ar-vr-developer.specialisationNote: AR/VR developers build immersive experiences. A bachelor in informatikk plus 3D/Unity skills. The field is small enough that portfolio and side-projects beat any specific degree.

art-director.specialisationNote: Art directors lead the visual concept on projects in advertising, film, or product. A bachelor in graphic design, visual communication, or fine art, plus several years as a designer first. Portfolio carries the role.

astronaut.specialisationNote: Astronauts require extensive training and skills similar to airline pilots, including technical mastery and teamwork in high-pressure environments.

athletic-trainer.specialisationNote: Athletic Trainers focus on injury management and prevention, which aligns closely with the rehabilitation and treatment aspects of Physiotherapy.

auditor.specialisationNote: Auditors verify the financial records of companies. A bachelor in accounting or business administration (NHH, BI, CBS), then 3 years of practical training to become statsautorisert revisor. Norwegian auditors are regulated by Finanstilsynet.

automation-engineer.specialisationNote: An Automation Engineer focuses on automating processes, similar to an Automation Technician who works with automation systems and technology.

aviation-engineer.specialisationNote: Aviation Engineers share a strong foundation in mechanical engineering principles, focusing on the design and development of aircraft systems.

backend-developer.specialisationNote: Backend developers build the server, database, and API layer of applications. Same education as a general software developer — a 3-year bachelor in informatikk/computer science, optionally a 2-year master. Specialisation comes from work experience and project portfolio, not the degree.

backend-engineer-distributed.specialisationNote: Distributed backend engineers build systems that scale across many servers. Same education as a general software developer — a bachelor in informatikk — plus deep experience with concurrency, message queues, and consensus protocols.

bailiff.specialisationNote: A Bailiff works closely with legal processes and enforcement, making a Law degree relevant for understanding the legal framework involved.

baker.specialisationNote: Bakers and chefs both work with food, requiring similar skills in timing and precision, but bakers focus specifically on baked goods.

bank-advisor.specialisationNote: A Bank Advisor works with financial products and customer service, similar to an Accountant who manages finances and provides financial advice.

bariatric-surgeon.specialisationNote: Bariatric surgery performs weight-loss operations. Complete general surgery specialisation, then a bariatric/metabolic surgery fellowship.

barista.specialisationNote: A barista shares skills with a chef in food and beverage preparation, focusing on quality and customer service in a café environment.

beautician.specialisationNote: Makeup artists and hairdressers both work in the beauty industry, focusing on enhancing clients' appearance and providing beauty services.

blockchain-developer.specialisationNote: Blockchain developers write smart contracts and decentralised applications. A bachelor in informatikk gives the foundation; the rest is self-taught around Solidity, Rust, and EVM tooling.

breast-surgeon.specialisationNote: Breast surgery focuses on breast cancer and reconstruction. Complete general surgery specialisation, then a breast surgery fellowship.

building-surveyor.specialisationNote: A Building Surveyor works closely with Civil Engineers to ensure buildings are safe and comply with regulations, making their education paths somewhat related.

business-analyst.specialisationNote: Business analysts translate data and processes into business decisions. A bachelor in business administration, economics, or informatikk works. The role values clear communication and SQL/Excel fluency over a specific degree.

butcher.specialisationNote: Butchers and chefs both work with meat, and a butcher's skills in preparing meat are essential for a chef's cooking.

cardiac-surgeon.specialisationNote: Cardiac surgery is a subspeciality of cardiothoracic surgery focusing on the heart. Medical degree + cardiothoracic specialisation + cardiac fellowship.

cardiothoracic-surgeon.specialisationNote: Cardiothoracic surgery covers the heart and lungs. After the medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in cardiothoracic surgery (8+ years total post-graduation).

career-coach.specialisationNote: A Career Coach often uses psychological principles to help clients understand their career paths, making a psychology background relevant.

caregiver.specialisationNote: A caregiver provides personal care and support similar to a care assistant, focusing on helping individuals with daily living activities.

case-worker.specialisationNote: Case workers manage individual welfare cases. A 3-year bachelor in sosialt arbeid (social work) or barnevernspedagogikk. OsloMet, NTNU, and Høgskulen i Volda all offer the programme.

catering-manager.specialisationNote: A Catering Manager often has a background as a Chef, overseeing food preparation and menu planning in various settings.

chartered-surveyor.specialisationNote: Chartered Surveyors assess land and buildings, similar to Civil Engineers who design and oversee construction projects, making their education paths somewhat related.

chief-financial-officer.specialisationNote: A Chief Financial Officer often has a background in accounting, as they manage financial strategies and reporting, which are core aspects of accounting.

chief-marine-engineer.specialisationNote: A Chief Marine Engineer's role involves mechanical systems and engineering principles, similar to those studied in mechanical engineering.

child-protection-officer.specialisationNote: Child protection officers (barnevernspedagog) safeguard children at risk. A 3-year bachelor in barnevernspedagogikk (child welfare). OsloMet and NTNU are the leading programmes.

chiropractor.specialisationNote: Chiropractors and physiotherapists both focus on treating musculoskeletal issues, using manual techniques and therapy to help patients recover and manage pain.

civil-defence-officer.specialisationNote: Civil Defence Officers work closely with firefighters during emergencies, providing support and coordination in disaster response situations.

civil-servant.specialisationNote: Civil servants often work with legal frameworks and policies, making a law degree relevant for understanding governance and administration.

clinical-researcher.specialisationNote: Clinical Researchers often have a medical background, similar to doctors, as they conduct trials that require deep medical knowledge and patient care understanding.

cloud-architect.specialisationNote: A Cloud Architect often works closely with Software Developers to design and implement cloud solutions, making their education paths quite similar in technology and programming.

cloud-engineer.specialisationNote: Cloud engineers design and run systems on AWS, Azure, or GCP. A bachelor in informatikk is the strongest entry; cloud certifications (AWS Solutions Architect, Azure Administrator) supplement the degree.

coast-guard-officer.specialisationNote: A Coast Guard Officer shares skills in leadership, decision-making, and public safety, similar to a Police Officer who also protects and serves the community.

coded-welder.specialisationNote: A Coded Welder is a specialised type of Welder who performs high-integrity welding, requiring additional certifications and skills beyond standard welding.

color-grading-specialist.specialisationNote: A Color Grading Specialist works with colour in film, similar to how a Painter & Decorator uses colour to enhance spaces, focusing on aesthetics.

colorectal-surgeon.specialisationNote: Colorectal surgery is a subspeciality of general surgery. Complete the medical degree + general surgery specialisation, then a colorectal fellowship (1-2 years).

combat-engineer.specialisationNote: Combat Engineers share skills in construction and engineering, making Civil Engineer a relevant base career in Norway.

commercial-real-estate-agent.specialisationNote: A Commercial Real Estate Agent often deals with legal aspects of property transactions, making knowledge of law beneficial in this field.

commissioning-contractor.specialisationNote: A Commissioning Contractor works closely with automation systems, making the role similar to that of an Automation Technician who also deals with system testing and implementation.

community-development-worker.specialisationNote: Community Development Workers often collaborate with social workers to support communities, making this a closely related field in Norway.

community-organiser.specialisationNote: Community organisers build local action and advocacy. A bachelor in sosialt arbeid, sosiologi, or statsvitenskap. The role values lived experience and network-building as much as the degree.

community-outreach-worker.specialisationNote: Community Outreach Workers often collaborate with Social Workers to support marginalised communities, making their roles closely related in community development.

compliance-director.specialisationNote: A Compliance Director often requires a strong legal background, similar to a Lawyer, focusing on regulatory compliance and policy development.

compressor-technician.specialisationNote: A Compressor Technician works with machinery similar to an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on maintaining and repairing compressors used in various industries.

computer-vision-engineer.specialisationNote: Computer vision engineers build systems that interpret images and video. A bachelor in informatikk plus a master in Machine Learning, Computer Science, or Electronics. NTNU has the strongest programmes in Norway.

confined-space-rescue-technician.specialisationNote: A Confined Space Rescue Technician often works alongside firefighters, providing rescue services in hazardous environments, which requires similar training in safety and emergency response.

construction-manager.specialisationNote: Construction Managers often work closely with Civil Engineers to ensure projects are completed effectively, making this a relevant educational path.

construction-program-director.specialisationNote: A Construction Program Director oversees building projects, similar to how a Civil Engineer designs and manages construction plans, making their education paths closely related.

construction-site-supervisor.specialisationNote: A Construction Site Supervisor's role closely aligns with that of a Civil Works Foreman, as both oversee construction activities and manage teams to ensure project success.

construction-worker.specialisationNote: A construction worker often collaborates with carpenters, as both roles involve building and working with materials on construction sites.

control-room-operator.specialisationNote: A Control Room Operator works closely with automation systems, making the skills and knowledge from an Automation Technician's education relevant and beneficial.

controlled-demolition-specialist.specialisationNote: A Controlled Demolition Specialist requires a background in civil engineering to understand structural integrity and demolition techniques, making this a closely related field.

corporate-lawyer.specialisationNote: Corporate lawyers advise companies on contracts, M&A, and compliance. Same education as any other lawyer — the 5-year master in rettsvitenskap. Corporate specialisation comes from working at a commercial law firm after graduating.

corrections-officer.specialisationNote: Corrections Officers work closely with inmates, similar to Social Workers who support individuals in need, focusing on rehabilitation and humane treatment.

cosmetic-surgeon.specialisationNote: Cosmetic surgery focuses on elective aesthetic procedures. Complete plastic surgery specialisation, then an aesthetic fellowship or private-practice training.

counsellor.specialisationNote: Counsellors provide talk-based support but do not have full clinical authorisation. The 6-year profesjonsstudium psykologi gives full authorisation; counsellor roles can also be entered via a bachelor + master in psychology, or a related field like sosialt arbeid.

crane-operator.specialisationNote: A Crane Operator works closely with construction teams, similar to a Civil Works Foreman, who oversees construction projects and ensures safety and efficiency.

crane-technician.specialisationNote: A Crane Technician's work involves mechanical systems, making a Mechanical Engineer a relevant base career due to overlapping skills in maintenance and repair.

crisis-intervention-specialist.specialisationNote: Crisis Intervention Specialists often work closely with Social Workers, addressing mental health crises and providing support in emergency situations.

critical-infrastructure-maintenance-tech.specialisationNote: A Critical Infrastructure Maintenance Technician works with electrical systems, similar to an Electrician, focusing on maintenance and repair in critical facilities.

cross-api-integration-architect.specialisationNote: An Integration Architect designs systems and APIs, which is closely related to the work of a Software Developer who also creates software solutions and integrations.

cross-network-automation-sw-eng.specialisationNote: A Network Automation Software Engineer writes software for automating network tasks, which is closely related to the skills and knowledge of a Software Developer.

cross-observability-eng.specialisationNote: An Observability Engineer works closely with software systems, requiring a strong foundation in software development and IT principles.

cross-oss-bss-product-mgr.specialisationNote: A Telecom Product Manager often works closely with software developers to create and manage telecom products, requiring a strong understanding of software practices.

cross-platform-product-owner.specialisationNote: The Telco Platform Product Owner works closely with software developers to manage product backlogs and ensure telecom needs are met, making software development knowledge essential.

cross-test-env-governance.specialisationNote: The role involves using data analytics to optimise test environments, which aligns closely with the skills of a data analyst in interpreting and managing data.

cto.specialisationNote: The Chief Technology Officer often oversees software development teams, making a background in software development highly relevant for this executive role.

customs-officer.specialisationNote: Customs Officers and Police Officers both enforce laws and regulations, requiring similar skills in observation and communication.

cyber-warfare-specialist.specialisationNote: A Cyber Warfare Specialist often requires skills in software development and cybersecurity to protect networks and conduct operations effectively.

data-architect.specialisationNote: A Data Architect designs data systems, while a Data Analyst interprets data; both roles require strong data skills and understanding of data management.

data-engineer.specialisationNote: Data engineers build the pipelines that move and transform data. A bachelor in informatikk or computer science plus experience with SQL, Python, and cloud data platforms (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks).

data-protection-officer.specialisationNote: A Data Protection Officer often requires legal knowledge, particularly in data protection laws like GDPR, making a law degree relevant.

data-scientist.specialisationNote: Data scientists build models and run experiments on data. A bachelor in statistics, mathematics, or informatikk, often followed by a master in Data Science or Machine Learning. NTNU and UiO both offer dedicated programmes.

database-administrator.specialisationNote: A Database Administrator works with data management and optimisation, similar to a Data Analyst who interprets and analyses data for insights.

decision-science-lead.specialisationNote: A Decision Science Lead uses data analysis to inform business decisions, similar to a Data Analyst who interprets data to provide insights.

dental-hygienist.specialisationNote: A Dental Hygienist works closely with dentists and dental assistants, focusing on patient care and oral health education, making this a relevant base career.

dermatologic-surgeon.specialisationNote: Dermatologic surgery treats skin cancers and conditions surgically. Complete dermatology specialisation, then a surgical dermatology fellowship.

detective.specialisationNote: Detectives are often police officers who specialise in investigating serious crimes, requiring similar training and skills in law enforcement.

devops-engineer.specialisationNote: DevOps engineers automate deployment and infrastructure. A bachelor in informatikk plus hands-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and tools like Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform. Often a transition from backend development.

devsecops-engineer.specialisationNote: A DevSecOps Engineer works closely with software developers to integrate security into the software development process, making this career closely related to software development.

dietitian.specialisationNote: Dietitians work within the healthcare field, focusing on nutrition and dietary plans, which aligns with the broader role of healthcare workers in patient care.

diplomat.specialisationNote: Diplomats often require legal knowledge to navigate international laws and treaties, making a law degree relevant for this career path.

director-of-engineering.specialisationNote: A Director of Engineering often oversees engineering projects, similar to how a Civil Engineer manages construction and infrastructure projects, requiring strong leadership and technical skills.

disability-support-worker.specialisationNote: Disability support workers (vernepleier) assist people with developmental disabilities. A 3-year bachelor in vernepleie. OsloMet and Høgskulen i Volda offer the programme.

district-heating-technician.specialisationNote: A District Heating Technician's work involves technical skills similar to those of a Mechanical Engineer, particularly in systems involving heat and energy.

dog-groomer.specialisationNote: A Dog Groomer works closely with animals, similar to a Veterinarian, but focuses on grooming rather than medical care.

domestic-violence-advocate.specialisationNote: Domestic Violence Advocates often work closely with Social Workers to support survivors, making their education paths quite similar in Norway.

drilling-technician.specialisationNote: A Drilling Technician operates complex machinery, similar to a Mechanical Engineer who designs and maintains such systems, focusing on technical skills and equipment management.

drone-operator-uav.specialisationNote: Drone operators share skills in piloting and situational awareness with helicopter pilots, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

early-childhood-educator.specialisationNote: Early Childhood Educators work closely with children, similar to Childcare Assistants, but with a greater focus on educational activities and development.

electrical-commissioning-technician.specialisationNote: An Electrical Commissioning Technician works closely with electrical systems, similar to an Electrician, focusing on testing and verifying installations.

electrical-contractor.specialisationNote: An Electrical Contractor is essentially a skilled Electrician who runs their own business, focusing on electrical installations and project management.

electrical-engineer.specialisationNote: Electrical Engineers design systems, while Electricians install and maintain electrical systems, making them closely related in the field of electrical work.

electro-technical-officer.specialisationNote: The Electro-Technical Officer's role involves managing automation systems, which closely relates to the skills of an Automation Technician in Norway.

embedded-developer.specialisationNote: Embedded developers write firmware for physical devices (cars, IoT, medical equipment). A bachelor in informatikk or electronics engineering, plus C/C++ and hardware experience. NTNU's Datateknologi or Elektronikk-systemer programme is the strongest route.

emergency-dispatcher.specialisationNote: Emergency Dispatchers play a crucial role in healthcare by coordinating responses to emergencies, making their work closely related to healthcare workers.

emt.specialisationNote: Emergency Medical Technicians provide crucial first-response care, which aligns with the broader role of healthcare workers in supporting patient health and safety.

endocrine-surgeon.specialisationNote: Endocrine surgery focuses on thyroid, parathyroid, and adrenal glands. Complete the medical degree + general surgery, then an endocrine surgery fellowship.

energy-commissioning-technician.specialisationNote: Energy Commissioning Technicians work with systems similar to Automation Technicians, focusing on testing and verifying energy systems during construction.

engineering-director.specialisationNote: An Industrial Engineering Director often oversees engineering projects similar to a Civil Engineer, focusing on technical excellence in infrastructure and manufacturing.

engineering-manager.specialisationNote: An Engineering Manager often comes from a background in software development, leading teams to create software products, making this a relevant base career.

enterprise-systems-consultant.specialisationNote: An Enterprise Systems Consultant often works closely with software developers to implement and optimise enterprise systems, making their education paths somewhat related.

eod-technician.specialisationNote: EOD Technicians often work alongside police officers to handle explosive threats, requiring similar skills in safety and precision under pressure.

equity-analyst.specialisationNote: An Equity Analyst uses financial data and analysis, similar to a Data Analyst who interprets data to inform decisions, making this a related field.

escalator-technician.specialisationNote: An Escalator Technician shares skills in mechanical systems and maintenance with an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on repair and servicing of machinery.

escrow-officer.specialisationNote: An Escrow Officer deals with legal documents and compliance in real estate, similar to a Lawyer who handles legal matters and documentation.

esports-analyst.specialisationNote: An Esports Analyst uses data analysis skills similar to a Data Analyst, focusing on game performance and strategies.

family-support-worker.specialisationNote: Family support workers help families in crisis. A bachelor in sosialt arbeid or barnevernspedagogikk. The barnevernspedagog route is the strongest fit for child-focused roles.

fashion-designer.specialisationNote: Fashion designers create clothing and accessories. A bachelor in fashion design — Konstfack (Stockholm) and KADK (Copenhagen) are the leading Nordic schools. Portfolio admission.

fast-food-crew.specialisationNote: A Fast Food Crew Member often works under a Chef, preparing and serving food in a restaurant setting, making this a related career path.

finance-director.specialisationNote: A Finance Director often has a background in accounting, as they manage financial planning and reporting, which are key aspects of accounting.

financial-advisor.specialisationNote: Financial Advisors and Accountants both require strong financial knowledge and analytical skills, making the educational paths somewhat similar in Norway.

financial-analyst.specialisationNote: A Financial Analyst often uses data analysis skills similar to those of a Data Analyst, focusing on financial data interpretation and forecasting.

fitness-instructor.specialisationNote: Personal trainers help clients improve their health and fitness, which aligns with the broader goals of healthcare workers in promoting well-being.

flight-attendant.specialisationNote: Both flight attendants and airline pilots work in the aviation industry, focusing on passenger safety and comfort during flights.

flight-engineer-space.specialisationNote: A Flight Engineer (Space) requires knowledge of systems and mechanics, similar to a Mechanical Engineer who designs and maintains machinery.

food-blogger.specialisationNote: A Food Blogger often shares recipes and cooking tips, which aligns closely with the skills and knowledge of a Chef in Norway.

food-product-developer.specialisationNote: A Food Product Developer works closely with chefs to create new food products, making culinary skills and creativity essential in both careers.

food-scientist.specialisationNote: Food Scientists study the properties of food, which is closely related to the work of Chefs who apply this knowledge in cooking and food preparation.

food-technologist.specialisationNote: A Food Technologist works on food production processes, while a Chef focuses on preparing and presenting food, both requiring knowledge of food safety and quality.

forensic-scientist.specialisationNote: Forensic Scientists provide crucial evidence that supports legal cases, making their work closely related to the legal field and lawyers who use this evidence in court.

forex-trader.specialisationNote: Forex trading involves financial analysis and risk management, which aligns with the skills needed in accounting, particularly in understanding market dynamics.

foster-care-coordinator.specialisationNote: A Foster Care Coordinator works closely with social workers to support children in foster care, making their roles closely related in child welfare.

fpso-technician.specialisationNote: An FPSO Technician's role in maintaining mechanical systems aligns closely with the skills and training of an Industrial Mechanic in Norway.

fractional-cto.specialisationNote: A Fractional CTO often has a background in software development, providing technical leadership and strategic guidance to tech companies.

frontend-developer.specialisationNote: Frontend developers build the user-facing layer of web and mobile apps. Same education as a general software developer — a bachelor in informatikk/computer science, plus self-study of frameworks like React or Vue. Portfolio matters more than the specific degree.

fullstack-engineer.specialisationNote: Full-stack engineers handle both frontend and backend. Same education as a general software developer — a bachelor in informatikk/computer science, then on-the-job exposure to both ends of the stack.

game-developer.specialisationNote: Game developers build games using engines like Unity or Unreal. A bachelor in informatikk gives the foundation; specialised game-design programmes exist at Westerdals and Høgskulen i Volda but a portfolio of shipped games matters more.

game-director.specialisationNote: A Game Director often collaborates closely with Software Developers to create and implement game features, making their education paths somewhat aligned.

gas-network-technician.specialisationNote: Gas Network Technicians work with systems that require similar technical skills and safety knowledge as Electricians, particularly in installation and maintenance.

gas-turbine-technician.specialisationNote: Gas Turbine Technicians work with complex machinery, similar to Mechanical Engineers who design and maintain such systems, making their education paths closely related.

general-counsel.specialisationNote: A General Counsel is a senior lawyer who oversees all legal matters in an organisation, making the role closely related to that of a lawyer in Norway.

general-practitioner.specialisationNote: After the 6-year medical degree, complete LIS1 (18 months) then the ALIS general-practice specialisation (5 years).

general-surgeon.specialisationNote: General surgery covers a broad range of abdominal and soft-tissue operations. After the 6-year medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in general surgery (6+ years).

graphic-designer.specialisationNote: Graphic designers create visual identity, print, and brand work. A bachelor in graphic design or visual communication. KHiO, Konstfack, and KADK are the leading Nordic schools — portfolio is the primary admission criterion.

grid-connection-technician.specialisationNote: A Grid Connection Technician works closely with electrical systems, similar to an Electrician, focusing on connecting power generation to the grid.

ground-control-operator.specialisationNote: Both Ground Control Operators and Airline Pilots are involved in the operation of aircraft, with Ground Control Operators managing communications and telemetry from the ground.

hand-surgeon.specialisationNote: Hand surgery covers the hand, wrist, and forearm. Accessible via orthopaedics or plastic surgery, then a hand surgery fellowship (1-2 years).

head-of-finance.specialisationNote: The Head of Finance role involves overseeing financial strategies, which closely relates to the work of an accountant who manages financial records and reporting.

head-of-legal.specialisationNote: The Head of Legal oversees legal matters, similar to a lawyer who provides legal advice and representation, making this a closely related career in Norway.

heavy-equipment-mechanic.specialisationNote: Heavy Equipment Mechanics share skills with Industrial Mechanics, focusing on servicing and repairing large machinery used in various industries.

helicopter-maintenance-engineer.specialisationNote: Helicopter Maintenance Engineers require mechanical engineering skills to maintain and repair helicopter systems, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

hepatobiliary-surgeon.specialisationNote: Hepatobiliary surgery focuses on liver, gallbladder, and pancreas. Complete the medical degree + general surgery, then a hepatobiliary fellowship (1-2 years).

high-voltage-electrician.specialisationNote: A High Voltage Electrician is a specialized type of Electrician who works with high-voltage systems, requiring additional training and safety certifications.

high-voltage-wind-technician.specialisationNote: A High Voltage Wind Technician is closely related to an Electrician, as both roles involve working with electrical systems and require similar training and skills.

homelessness-support-worker.specialisationNote: Homelessness support workers help rough sleepers access housing and services. A bachelor in sosialt arbeid is the strongest route; field experience and resilience matter equally.

housing-support-officer.specialisationNote: Housing Support Officers often work closely with social workers to assist individuals in securing and maintaining housing, making their roles complementary in supporting vulnerable populations.

hr-specialist.specialisationNote: HR Specialists and Social Workers both focus on understanding and supporting people, making their skills in communication and empathy valuable in both fields.

hvac-technician.specialisationNote: HVAC Technicians often work with electrical systems, making skills in electricity essential, similar to the training of Electricians in Norway.

hydraulic-systems-technician.specialisationNote: A Hydraulic Systems Technician works with hydraulic systems, which is a key part of industrial mechanics, focusing on installation and maintenance of machinery.

iac-specialist.specialisationNote: An Infrastructure as Code Specialist works closely with software development practices, focusing on automating infrastructure management through coding techniques.

illustrator.specialisationNote: Illustrators create commissioned artwork for editorial, books, and brands. A bachelor in illustration, visual communication, or fine art. KHiO and Konstfack are the leading Nordic schools. Portfolio is everything.

immigration-officer.specialisationNote: An Immigration Officer often requires knowledge of laws and regulations, similar to a Lawyer, making this a relevant educational path in Norway.

independent-contractor-technical.specialisationNote: As a Senior Technical Contractor, you would likely have skills in software development, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

industrial-abseiler.specialisationNote: An Industrial Abseiler often performs painting and coating tasks on tall structures, which aligns with the skills of a Painter & Decorator.

industrial-fitter.specialisationNote: An Industrial Fitter's work closely aligns with that of an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on the assembly and maintenance of mechanical systems in industrial settings.

industrial-instrumentation-technician.specialisationNote: An Industrial Instrumentation Technician focuses on maintaining instrumentation systems, which is closely related to the role of an Automation Technician who works with automated systems.

industrial-maintenance-supervisor.specialisationNote: An Industrial Maintenance Supervisor oversees maintenance, similar to an Industrial Mechanic who repairs and maintains machinery, making their education paths closely related.

infantry-officer.specialisationNote: Both Infantry Officers and Police Officers lead teams and make critical decisions in high-pressure situations, focusing on safety and security.

infrastructure-program-director.specialisationNote: An Infrastructure Program Director often collaborates with civil engineers on large-scale projects, making this a relevant educational path in Norway.

instrumentation-contractor.specialisationNote: An Instrumentation Contractor works closely with automation systems, making the Automation Technician a relevant base career due to similar skills in control systems and instrumentation.

instrumentation-technician.specialisationNote: An Instrumentation Technician works with measurement and control instruments, similar to an Automation Technician who focuses on automated systems and processes in industry.

insurance-advisor.specialisationNote: Insurance Advisors often require strong financial knowledge similar to Accountants, as they help clients manage financial risks through insurance products.

interior-designer.specialisationNote: Interior designers shape physical spaces. A bachelor in interior architecture or interior design. KHiO, AHO, and KADK all offer relevant programmes.

investment-analyst.specialisationNote: Investment Analysts often use data analysis skills to evaluate investment opportunities, making the Data Analyst career a relevant base in Norway.

investment-banker.specialisationNote: Investment bankers and accountants both work with financial data, but investment bankers focus more on advising companies and deal-making.

investment-manager.specialisationNote: Investment Managers often require strong financial analysis skills similar to those of Accountants, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

journalist.specialisationNote: Journalists often work closely with legal matters, requiring an understanding of laws and ethics, similar to the training of a lawyer.

judge.specialisationNote: Judges are often experienced lawyers who have advanced their careers to preside over court cases, making the legal education path similar.

key-account-director.specialisationNote: A Key Account Director manages client relationships and strategies, similar to how an Accountant manages financial accounts and client financial strategies.

kindergarten-teacher.specialisationNote: Kindergarten teachers (barnehagelærer) work with children aged 1-5. A 3-year bachelor in barnehagelærerutdanning. OsloMet, DMMH (Trondheim), and several university colleges offer it.

kitchen-porter.specialisationNote: A Kitchen Porter supports chefs by preparing ingredients and maintaining kitchen hygiene, making it a foundational role in the culinary field.

lab-technician.specialisationNote: Lab Technicians perform tests that support healthcare workers in diagnosing and treating patients, making their roles closely related in the medical field.

land-surveyor.specialisationNote: Land Surveyors often work closely with Civil Engineers to determine property boundaries and ensure accurate land development.

launch-operations-engineer.specialisationNote: A Launch Operations Engineer and an Airline Pilot both work in the aerospace field, focusing on safety and coordination during critical operations.

licensed-aircraft-engineer.specialisationNote: Licensed Aircraft Engineers and Mechanical Engineers both require strong technical skills and knowledge of complex systems, making their education paths somewhat similar.

lift-technician.specialisationNote: Lift Technicians work with electrical systems and mechanical components, similar to Electricians, focusing on lift installations and repairs.

lighting-technician.specialisationNote: Lighting Technicians often work with electrical systems and lighting setups, making skills in electrics and safety relevant to the Electrician career.

loan-officer.specialisationNote: Loan Officers assess financial applications, similar to how Accountants evaluate financial data and ensure compliance with regulations.

ma-director.specialisationNote: An M&A Director often works closely with lawyers to navigate legal aspects of mergers and acquisitions, making a law background beneficial.

machine-learning-engineer.specialisationNote: ML engineers train and deploy machine-learning models. A bachelor in informatikk/computer science or statistics, often followed by a master in Machine Learning or Data Science. NTNU's MIT (Master in Informatics) is a leading route.

maintenance-release-engineer.specialisationNote: A Maintenance Release Engineer ensures aircraft safety and compliance, similar to how a Mechanical Engineer focuses on the design and maintenance of mechanical systems.

maintenance-shutdown-contractor.specialisationNote: A Maintenance Shutdown Contractor often works closely with mechanical engineers to plan and execute maintenance tasks in industrial settings, ensuring equipment operates efficiently.

marine-electrician.specialisationNote: A Marine Electrician is a specialised type of Electrician who works specifically on ships and offshore vessels, focusing on marine electrical systems.

marine-engineer.specialisationNote: Marine engineers design and maintain ship machinery. A bachelor or master in marin teknikk or maskinteknikk with marine specialisation. NTNU's Marin teknikk programme is world-leading; HVL (Bergen) offers a vocational route.

market-analyst.specialisationNote: A Market Analyst uses data analysis skills similar to a Data Analyst, focusing on financial markets instead of general data.

massage-therapist.specialisationNote: Massage therapists often work alongside physiotherapists, using similar techniques to help patients recover and improve their physical wellbeing.

mechanical-commissioning-technician.specialisationNote: A Mechanical Commissioning Technician works closely with mechanical systems, similar to an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on testing and operational readiness.

mechanical-maintenance-contractor.specialisationNote: Mechanical Maintenance Contractors focus on maintaining and repairing industrial machinery, which aligns closely with the skills and training of Industrial Mechanics in Norway.

medical-affairs-director.specialisationNote: A Medical Affairs Director often has a medical degree and works closely with clinical science, making the role closely related to that of a doctor.

medical-secretary.specialisationNote: A Medical Secretary provides essential administrative support in healthcare settings, making it closely related to the broader role of a Healthcare Worker.

mental-health-nurse.specialisationNote: A Mental Health Nurse is a specialist nurse who focuses on caring for individuals with mental health issues, building on the general nursing education.

mental-health-support-worker.specialisationNote: Mental Health Support Workers provide essential support within the healthcare sector, focusing on mental health, which aligns closely with the role of a Healthcare Worker.

midwife.specialisationNote: Midwives (jordmor) deliver babies and care for mothers. First, complete the 3-year bachelor in sykepleie (nursing) and gain authorisation; then complete a 2-year master in jordmorfag. OsloMet, UiB, and NTNU all offer the master.

military-officer.specialisationNote: Both military officers and police officers lead teams and maintain order, requiring strong leadership and decision-making skills.

military-pilot.specialisationNote: Military pilots often fly helicopters, making this career closely related in terms of skills and training required.

military-police.specialisationNote: Military Police share similarities with Police Officers in law enforcement and investigation, focusing on maintaining order and security within military contexts.

millwright.specialisationNote: A Millwright's work closely aligns with that of an Industrial Mechanic, as both involve installing and maintaining heavy machinery in industrial settings.

mlops-engineer.specialisationNote: MLOps Engineers work closely with software developers to create and maintain the systems that deploy machine learning models effectively.

mobile-developer.specialisationNote: Mobile developers build iOS and Android apps. Same education as a general software developer — a bachelor in informatikk, then platform-specific learning (Swift/SwiftUI for iOS, Kotlin for Android).

mortgage-advisor.specialisationNote: A Mortgage Advisor provides financial advice similar to an Accountant, focusing on helping clients manage their finances and understand financial products.

music-licensing-specialist.specialisationNote: A Music Licensing Specialist often deals with copyright law and contracts, making a law degree relevant for understanding legal aspects in the music industry.

music-teacher.specialisationNote: Music teachers in Norwegian schools need a teaching degree (PPU) on top of a bachelor in music. NMH (Norges musikkhøgskole) and the Grieg Academy in Bergen are the leading routes.

music-therapist.specialisationNote: Music therapists use music in clinical settings. A bachelor in music plus a master in music therapy. NMH (Oslo) and Universitetet i Bergen both offer the master.

nail-technician.specialisationNote: A Nail Technician shares skills in beauty and customer service with Hairdressers, focusing on enhancing clients' appearance.

nav-case-worker.specialisationNote: NAV Case Workers provide support similar to Social Workers, helping citizens with welfare and social services, requiring similar skills and educational backgrounds.

naval-officer.specialisationNote: Both Naval Officers and Airline Pilots require strong leadership and navigation skills, although their environments differ significantly.

neurosurgeon.specialisationNote: Neurosurgery covers the brain, spinal cord, and nervous system. After the medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in neurosurgery (7+ years).

nlp-engineer.specialisationNote: NLP engineers build language-processing systems. A bachelor in informatikk plus a master with focus on machine learning or computational linguistics. UiO's Informatics programme has an NLP track.

non-destructive-testing-technician.specialisationNote: Non-Destructive Testing Technicians often work closely with Mechanical Engineers to ensure the integrity of structures and components, using similar technical skills and knowledge.

nurse-practitioner.specialisationNote: A Nurse Practitioner is an advanced Nurse who has additional training to diagnose and treat patients, building on the foundational skills of a registered Nurse.

obstetric-gynaecological-surgeon.specialisationNote: OB/GYN surgery covers female reproductive health. After the medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in obstetrics and gynaecology (5+ years).

occupational-therapist.specialisationNote: Occupational Therapists work within the healthcare field, helping individuals recover and adapt to daily activities after health challenges, similar to other healthcare roles.

offensive-security-engineer.specialisationNote: Offensive Security Engineers often have a background in software development, as they need to understand coding and software vulnerabilities to perform their tasks effectively.

offshore-electrician.specialisationNote: An Offshore Electrician is a specialised type of Electrician who works in challenging environments like oil and gas platforms, focusing on safety and reliability.

offshore-instrumentation-technician.specialisationNote: An Offshore Instrumentation Technician works with control systems, similar to an Automation Technician who also focuses on automation and instrumentation in various settings.

offshore-maintenance-technician.specialisationNote: Offshore Maintenance Technicians perform maintenance similar to Industrial Mechanics, focusing on ensuring machinery operates efficiently in offshore environments.

offshore-mechanical-technician.specialisationNote: An Offshore Mechanical Technician performs similar mechanical maintenance tasks as an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on equipment and systems in offshore environments.

offshore-production-technician.specialisationNote: An Offshore Production Technician works with automated systems in oil and gas production, similar to an Automation Technician who focuses on control systems and process optimisation.

offshore-service-contractor.specialisationNote: Offshore Service Contractors often require mechanical engineering skills for maintenance and modifications on installations, linking the two careers closely.

offshore-wind-technician.specialisationNote: Offshore Wind Technicians often have a background in electrical trades, as they perform maintenance on wind turbine electrical systems.

oncological-surgeon.specialisationNote: Surgical oncology removes cancerous tumours. Complete general surgery specialisation, then a surgical oncology fellowship (2+ years).

ophthalmic-surgeon.specialisationNote: Ophthalmic surgery covers the eye. After the medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in ophthalmology (5+ years).

ophthalmologist.specialisationNote: An ophthalmologist is a type of doctor who focuses specifically on eye health and surgery, requiring a medical degree and additional training in ophthalmology.

oral-maxillofacial-surgeon.specialisationNote: Maxillofacial surgery requires BOTH a medical degree AND a dental degree, followed by a maxillofacial surgery specialisation (4+ years).

orthopaedic-surgeon.specialisationNote: Orthopaedic surgery covers bones, joints, and muscles. After the medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in orthopaedic surgery (6+ years).

otolaryngologist.specialisationNote: ENT surgery covers the ear, nose, throat, and head/neck. After the medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in otorhinolaryngology (5+ years).

outreach-worker.specialisationNote: Outreach workers connect with people who don't access traditional services (homeless, addiction, marginalised youth). A bachelor in sosialt arbeid or vernepleie, often combined with field experience.

overhead-line-equipment-technician.specialisationNote: An Overhead Line Equipment Technician works with electrical systems similar to an Electrician, focusing on high-voltage installations for railway power supply.

paediatric-surgeon.specialisationNote: Paediatric surgery operates on children. Complete general surgery specialisation, then a paediatric surgery fellowship (2+ years).

painter.specialisationNote: A Painter & Decorator focuses on applying finishes like paint and wallpaper, which is closely related to the work of a Painter.

paramedic.specialisationNote: Paramedics are healthcare workers who provide emergency medical care, making this a closely related field with similar skills and training requirements.

pastry-chef.specialisationNote: A Pastry Chef is a specialised type of Chef focusing on desserts and pastries, requiring similar culinary skills and training.

pathologist.specialisationNote: A pathologist is a type of doctor who focuses on diagnosing diseases through laboratory tests, making their education and skills closely aligned with general medical training.

pediatrician.specialisationNote: After the 6-year medical degree, complete LIS1 (18 months) then LIS2/3 paediatrics specialisation (5 years).

perf-test-engineer.specialisationNote: Load Test Engineers work closely with Software Developers to ensure applications perform well under stress, making their roles interconnected in software quality assurance.

performance-analyst.specialisationNote: A Performance Analyst uses data analysis skills similar to a Data Analyst, focusing on sports performance metrics and trends.

peripheral-nerve-surgeon.specialisationNote: Peripheral nerve surgery repairs damaged nerves. Accessible via neurosurgery or plastic surgery, then a peripheral nerve fellowship.

petroleum-engineer.specialisationNote: Petroleum engineers extract oil and gas. A 5-year master in petroleumsfag — NTNU, UiS (Stavanger), and UiB all offer it. The Norwegian energy transition is reshaping the role toward CCS and offshore wind.

pharmacy-technician.specialisationNote: Pharmacy technicians support healthcare workers by managing medications and assisting patients, making their roles closely related in the healthcare field.

pipefitter.specialisationNote: A Pipefitter's work involves installing piping systems, which is closely related to the skills of an Industrial Mechanic who also works with machinery and systems in industrial settings.

plastic-surgeon.specialisationNote: Plastic surgery covers reconstruction and repair. After the medical degree + LIS1, complete LIS2/3 in plastic surgery (6+ years).

platform-architect.specialisationNote: A Platform Architect designs systems for developers, similar to how a Software Developer creates software applications, making their education paths closely related.

platform-director-media.specialisationNote: A Streaming Platform Director requires strong software development skills to lead platform strategy and engineering for media products, making this career closely related.

platform-engineer.specialisationNote: Platform engineers build internal tooling and infrastructure that other developers use. A bachelor in informatikk plus systems-design experience. Usually a transition from backend or DevOps.

plc-technician.specialisationNote: A PLC Technician is closely related to an Automation Technician, as both roles involve working with automated systems and programming in industrial settings.

plumber.specialisationNote: Both plumbers and electricians work with essential systems in buildings, requiring technical skills and vocational training.

pneumatic-systems-technician.specialisationNote: A Pneumatic Systems Technician works with systems that are part of industrial mechanics, focusing on pneumatic control systems and compressed air networks.

podiatrist.specialisationNote: A podiatrist is a specialist doctor focusing on foot and lower limb health, making their education and skills closely related to general medicine.

port-crane-technician.specialisationNote: A Port Crane Technician shares skills in mechanical systems and maintenance with an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on heavy machinery used in ports.

power-line-technician.specialisationNote: Power Line Technicians work with electrical systems, similar to Electricians, focusing on power transmission and distribution.

power-plant-technician.specialisationNote: A Power Plant Technician's role in maintaining and operating machinery is closely related to the skills of an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on mechanical systems.

precision-machinist.specialisationNote: A Precision Machinist closely relates to a CNC Machinist, as both roles involve advanced machining techniques and producing components with tight tolerances.

primary-teacher.specialisationNote: Primary teachers (grunnskolelærer 1.-7. trinn) teach years 1-7. A 5-year integrated master in grunnskolelærerutdanning. Most Norwegian universities offer the programme.

principal-data-scientist.specialisationNote: A Principal Data Scientist's role involves advanced data analysis and strategy, which closely relates to the work of a Data Analyst in handling data and deriving insights.

principal-engineer.specialisationNote: A Principal Engineer sets technical direction and makes architectural decisions, similar to how a Civil Engineer designs and oversees construction projects.

private-equity-associate.specialisationNote: A Private Equity Associate often requires strong financial skills similar to those of an Accountant, particularly in financial modelling and valuation.

probation-officer.specialisationNote: Probation Officers work closely with individuals in the community, similar to Social Workers who also focus on rehabilitation and support.

process-operator.specialisationNote: Process Operators work closely with industrial machinery, making the Industrial Mechanic career a relevant match due to shared technical skills and knowledge in manufacturing processes.

product-manager.specialisationNote: A Product Manager often works closely with Software Developers to create and improve products, making their paths somewhat related in technology and project management.

product-manager-tech.specialisationNote: A Technical Product Manager works closely with software developers to define product vision and requirements, making this role closely related to software development.

product-owner.specialisationNote: A Product Owner works closely with software developers to manage product features and priorities, making this role closely related to software development.

property-consultant.specialisationNote: A Property Consultant often deals with legal aspects of property transactions, making knowledge of law beneficial, similar to a Lawyer's expertise.

property-developer.specialisationNote: Property Developers often work closely with Civil Engineers to plan and execute construction projects, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

property-lawyer.specialisationNote: A Property Lawyer is a type of Lawyer who focuses specifically on legal issues related to real estate transactions and property law.

prosecutor.specialisationNote: A Prosecutor is a type of Lawyer who specifically represents the state in criminal cases, requiring similar legal education and skills.

protection-and-control-technician.specialisationNote: A Protection and Control Technician works with systems that automate and protect electrical networks, similar to the role of an Automation Technician who focuses on automated systems.

psychiatrist.specialisationNote: After the 6-year medical degree, complete LIS1 (18 months) then LIS2/3 psychiatry specialisation (5 years).

public-health-social-worker.specialisationNote: Public Health Social Workers focus on health issues within communities, which aligns closely with the broader role of Social Workers who address various social challenges.

public-health-specialist.specialisationNote: Public Health Specialists often work on community health initiatives, similar to Social Workers who support individuals and communities in need.

pump-technician.specialisationNote: A Pump Technician's work in installing and maintaining pumps aligns closely with the skills and training of an Industrial Mechanic, focusing on mechanical systems.

qa-engineer.specialisationNote: QA engineers build automated test suites and validate software releases. A bachelor in informatikk gives the strongest entry; the role rewards systematic thinking and tool-fluency over deep theory.

quant-developer.specialisationNote: Quant Developers create software for trading systems, making their work closely related to Software Developers who also build applications and systems.

quantitative-analyst.specialisationNote: Quants build mathematical models for finance. A bachelor in mathematics, statistics, or physics, almost always followed by a master in Quantitative Finance, Financial Mathematics, or similar. NHH and BI offer relevant masters in Norway.

quantity-surveyor.specialisationNote: A Quantity Surveyor works closely with Civil Engineers on construction projects, focusing on cost management while engineers handle design and structural aspects.

radiographer.specialisationNote: Radiographers are healthcare workers who specialise in using imaging technology to assist in diagnosing patients, making their role closely related to general healthcare work.

radiologist.specialisationNote: After the 6-year medical degree, complete LIS1 (18 months) then LIS2/3 radiology specialisation (5 years).

rail-signalling-technician.specialisationNote: Rail Signalling Technicians work with electrical systems similar to Electricians, focusing on railway signalling systems and safety-critical wiring.

rail-systems-technician.specialisationNote: Rail Systems Technicians work with electrical systems similar to Industrial Electricians, focusing on railway infrastructure and maintenance.

real-estate-agent.specialisationNote: Real Estate Agents often work closely with lawyers to handle property transactions and contracts, making legal knowledge beneficial in this field.

real-estate-appraiser.specialisationNote: Real Estate Appraisers assess property values, similar to how Accountants evaluate financial data, both requiring strong analytical skills and attention to detail.

real-estate-broker.specialisationNote: Real estate brokers often deal with legal aspects of property transactions, making a background in law beneficial for understanding contracts and compliance.

real-estate-consultant.specialisationNote: Real Estate Consultants often provide legal advice related to property transactions, making a law background beneficial for this career.

real-estate-developer.specialisationNote: Real Estate Developers often work closely with Civil Engineers to ensure that property developments are structurally sound and meet regulatory standards.

real-estate-investor.specialisationNote: Real Estate Investors often require strong financial analysis skills, similar to accountants, to manage property finances effectively.

reconstructive-surgeon.specialisationNote: Reconstructive surgery restores form after trauma or cancer. Complete plastic surgery specialisation, then a reconstructive fellowship.

referee.specialisationNote: Referees enforce rules and make quick decisions, similar to police officers who maintain order and ensure rules are followed in society.

refuge-worker.specialisationNote: Refuge Workers often have similar training and responsibilities to Social Workers, focusing on providing support to vulnerable populations.

registered-nurse.specialisationNote: A Registered Nurse is a specialised type of Nurse who provides direct patient care and coordinates treatment in healthcare settings.

regulatory-affairs-director.specialisationNote: A Regulatory Affairs Director often needs legal knowledge to navigate regulations, making a law degree relevant for understanding compliance and policy.

rehabilitation-worker.specialisationNote: Rehabilitation Workers often collaborate with Social Workers to support individuals in their recovery, making their educational paths closely related.

renewable-energy-tech.specialisationNote: Renewable Energy Technicians often require electrical knowledge and skills similar to those of electricians, as they work with energy systems.

residential-care-worker.specialisationNote: A Residential Care Worker often collaborates with Social Workers to provide care and support to individuals in need, sharing similar educational paths and skills.

residential-real-estate-agent.specialisationNote: A Residential Real Estate Agent often deals with legal aspects of property transactions, making knowledge of law beneficial in this career.

restaurant-manager.specialisationNote: A Restaurant Manager often has a background in culinary arts, making the Chef role closely related as both involve food service and management.

restaurant-owner.specialisationNote: A restaurant owner often works closely with chefs to create menus and manage kitchen operations, making culinary skills essential for success.

restaurant-server.specialisationNote: A restaurant server works closely with chefs to deliver food, making their role essential in the dining experience, which is why this career is related.

rig-electrician.specialisationNote: A Rig Electrician is a specialised type of Electrician who works on drilling rigs, focusing on power systems and safety in offshore environments.

rig-instrumentation-technician.specialisationNote: A Rig Instrumentation Technician works with control systems and instrumentation, similar to an Automation Technician who also focuses on automated systems and controls.

risk-analyst.specialisationNote: A Risk Analyst uses data analysis skills similar to a Data Analyst, focusing on identifying and quantifying risks in various business areas.

risk-and-compliance-director.specialisationNote: A Risk and Compliance Director often requires a strong understanding of legal frameworks, making a law degree relevant for this role in Norway.

robotics-engineer.specialisationNote: Robotics Engineers often have a background in Mechanical Engineering, focusing on the design and integration of robotic systems.

rocket-scientist.specialisationNote: Rocket scientists often require mechanical engineering principles to design propulsion systems, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

rope-access-technician.specialisationNote: Rope Access Technicians often require welding skills for maintenance tasks, making a welding background relevant to their work at heights.

rotating-equipment-specialist.specialisationNote: A Rotating Equipment Specialist focuses on maintaining and analysing rotating machinery, which aligns closely with the skills and tasks of an Industrial Mechanic in Norway.

rov-pilot-technician.specialisationNote: An ROV Pilot Technician operates complex machinery and systems, similar to the skills required for an Automation Technician, particularly in electronics and maintenance.

safeguarding-officer.specialisationNote: A Safeguarding Officer works closely with social workers to protect vulnerable individuals, often requiring similar training and knowledge in social work principles.

satellite-engineer.specialisationNote: Satellite Engineers often require skills in mechanical engineering for designing satellite structures and systems, making this a relevant base career.

saturation-diver.specialisationNote: Saturation divers often perform deep-sea welding tasks, making skills in welding essential for their work in underwater environments.

scada-technician.specialisationNote: SCADA Technicians work with automation systems, making the Automation Technician a closely related field with similar technical skills and training requirements.

school-counsellor.specialisationNote: School Counsellors often work alongside Social Workers to support students' emotional and social needs, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

scouting-analyst.specialisationNote: A Scouting Analyst uses data analysis skills similar to a Data Analyst, focusing on player performance and recruitment rather than general data.

search-and-rescue-operator.specialisationNote: Search and Rescue Operators often work alongside firefighters and share skills in emergency response and rescue techniques, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

secondary-teacher.specialisationNote: Lower-secondary teachers (grunnskolelærer 5.-10. trinn) teach years 5-10. A 5-year integrated master in grunnskolelærerutdanning with subject specialisations. NTNU, OsloMet, UiB all offer it.

security-engineer.specialisationNote: Security engineers protect systems from attacks. A bachelor in informatikk is the entry point; many add a master in Information Security. NTNU's Information Security master is the leading Norwegian programme.

security-guard.specialisationNote: A Security Guard's role in protecting people and property shares similarities with a Police Officer's duties in maintaining safety and responding to incidents.

senior-data-engineer.specialisationNote: A Senior Data Engineer often requires software development skills to build data pipelines and systems, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

senior-data-scientist.specialisationNote: A Senior Data Scientist's role involves advanced data analysis and predictive modelling, which closely relates to the work of a Data Analyst in interpreting data for decision-making.

senior-devops-engineer.specialisationNote: A Senior DevOps Engineer works closely with Software Developers to improve deployment processes and automate infrastructure, making this a relevant career connection in Norway.

senior-legal-counsel.specialisationNote: A Senior Legal Counsel is a highly experienced lawyer who provides advanced legal advice and manages complex legal issues within an organisation.

senior-software-engineer.specialisationNote: A Senior Software Engineer is an advanced role within software development, focusing on high-quality code and mentoring, similar to a Software Developer's path.

senior-sre.specialisationNote: A Senior Site Reliability Engineer works closely with software developers to ensure applications run smoothly and reliably, making this a relevant career match.

senior-wind-turbine-technician.specialisationNote: A Senior Wind Turbine Technician requires mechanical skills and knowledge similar to those of a Mechanical Engineer, particularly in turbine repair and diagnostics.

ships-electrical-engineer.specialisationNote: A Ship's Electrical Engineer requires skills in electrical systems similar to those of an Electrician, focusing on maritime applications and safety.

shipyard-commissioning-technician.specialisationNote: A Shipyard Commissioning Technician works with automation systems, similar to an Automation Technician who focuses on installing and maintaining automated systems in various industries.

site-manager.specialisationNote: A Construction Site Manager oversees building projects, similar to a Civil Works Foreman who manages construction activities and ensures safety and efficiency on site.

site-reliability-engineer.specialisationNote: Site Reliability Engineers keep large systems running. A bachelor in informatikk plus deep experience with distributed systems, observability, and incident response. Usually a transition role from backend or DevOps.

sniper.specialisationNote: A sniper often works in law enforcement or military contexts, similar to police officers who require precision and tactical skills in their roles.

social-services-manager.specialisationNote: Social Services Managers often oversee teams of social workers, making their educational paths and responsibilities closely related.

software-architect.specialisationNote: A Software Architect designs the overall structure of software systems, while a Software Developer builds those systems, making them closely related in the tech field.

soldier.specialisationNote: Both soldiers and police officers serve to protect and defend the public, requiring similar skills in discipline, teamwork, and physical fitness.

solutions-architect.specialisationNote: A Solutions Architect designs systems that software developers build, making their roles closely related in the tech industry.

sommelier.specialisationNote: A sommelier works closely with chefs to select and pair wines with dishes, making their roles complementary in fine dining establishments.

space-robotics-engineer.specialisationNote: Space Robotics Engineers often require mechanical engineering skills to design and operate robotic systems used in space exploration.

space-systems-engineer.specialisationNote: A Space Systems Engineer requires strong engineering skills, similar to those of a Mechanical Engineer, focusing on system integration and design.

spacecraft-pilot.specialisationNote: Spacecraft pilots share similar skills with airline pilots, such as flight operations and decision-making under pressure, making this a relevant base career.

special-forces-operator.specialisationNote: Special Forces Operators and Police Officers both require elite training and skills for high-pressure situations, focusing on safety and security.

special-needs-assistant.specialisationNote: A Special Needs Assistant works closely with children, similar to a Childcare Assistant, focusing on supporting their learning and development.

special-needs-educator.specialisationNote: A Special Needs Educator often works closely with social workers to support students with special needs, making this a relevant related field in Norway.

speech-language-therapist.specialisationNote: Speech and Language Therapists work within the healthcare field, focusing on communication and swallowing difficulties, which aligns with the broader role of healthcare workers.

spinal-surgeon.specialisationNote: Spinal surgery is a subspeciality accessible via orthopaedics or neurosurgery. Complete the base specialisation, then a spine fellowship (1-2 years).

sports-agent.specialisationNote: A Sports Agent often requires a background in law to negotiate contracts and understand legal aspects, making a lawyer a closely related career in Norway.

sports-analyst.specialisationNote: A Sports Analyst uses data analysis skills similar to a Data Analyst, focusing on sports performance rather than general data.

sports-medicine-surgeon.specialisationNote: Sports medicine surgery focuses on athletic injuries. Complete orthopaedic surgery specialisation, then a sports medicine fellowship (1-2 years).

sports-physiotherapist.specialisationNote: A Sports Physiotherapist is a specialised type of Physiotherapist who focuses on treating athletes, using similar skills and education.

sports-psychologist.specialisationNote: A Sports Psychologist is a type of Psychologist who focuses on helping athletes improve their mental performance and cope with the pressures of sports.

sports-therapist.specialisationNote: Sports Therapists focus on rehabilitation and injury management, similar to Physiotherapists who also help patients recover from injuries and improve physical function.

sre.specialisationNote: Site Reliability Engineers keep large systems running. A bachelor in informatikk plus deep experience with distributed systems, observability, and incident response. Usually a transition role from backend or DevOps.

staff-engineer.specialisationNote: A Staff Engineer often has a background in software development, focusing on solving complex technical issues and improving engineering practices.

steam-turbine-technician.specialisationNote: A Steam Turbine Technician works with mechanical systems, similar to a Mechanical Engineer, focusing on maintenance and repair of complex machinery.

stockbroker.specialisationNote: Stockbrokers require strong financial knowledge and skills similar to accountants, as both roles involve managing financial information and advising clients.

submarine-officer.specialisationNote: Both submarine officers and airline pilots require navigation skills and teamwork, although one operates underwater and the other in the air.

subsea-controls-technician.specialisationNote: A Subsea Controls Technician works with automation systems, similar to an Automation Technician, focusing on control systems for subsea equipment.

subsea-technician.specialisationNote: A Subsea Technician works with complex machinery and systems, similar to a Mechanical Engineer who designs and maintains mechanical systems.

substance-abuse-counsellor.specialisationNote: Substance Abuse Counsellors often work closely with Social Workers, as both roles involve helping individuals overcome personal challenges and improve their well-being.

substation-technician.specialisationNote: A Substation Technician works with electrical systems, similar to an Electrician, focusing on voltage transformation and maintenance of power distribution equipment.

support-worker.specialisationNote: Support Workers and Care Assistants both provide essential support to individuals with various needs, focusing on improving their quality of life and independence.

surgeon.specialisationNote: Surgery is a specialisation you choose after completing a 6-year medical degree and an 18-month internship. Surgical training then takes around 6 more years.

surveyor.specialisationNote: Surveyors work closely with civil engineers to ensure land measurements and mapping are accurate for construction projects, making their education paths somewhat related.

systems-architect.specialisationNote: A Systems Architect designs complex systems, often requiring software development skills, making this a relevant base career in Norway.

systems-engineer.specialisationNote: A Systems Engineer often works closely with software development, focusing on integrating and maintaining systems that include software components.

tank-commander.specialisationNote: Both Tank Commanders and Police Officers require strong leadership and tactical skills, often working in high-pressure situations to ensure safety and effective operations.

tax-inspector.specialisationNote: Tax Inspectors often have a background in accounting, as they need to understand tax laws and financial statements to perform their audits effectively.

technical-architect.specialisationNote: A Technical Architect designs the overall software structure, while a Software Developer builds the actual software, making their education paths closely related.

technical-director.specialisationNote: A Technical Director often oversees software development projects, making their role closely related to that of a Software Developer, focusing on technical leadership and architecture.

technical-director-gaming.specialisationNote: A Gaming Technical Director's role involves software development skills, particularly in game engines and tools, making it closely related to software development.

technology-transformation-lead.specialisationNote: A Technology Transformation Lead often works closely with software developers to modernise and migrate technology platforms, requiring a strong understanding of software development principles.

telco-ai-ml-engineer.specialisationNote: AI and ML Engineers often work with software development, using programming skills to create machine learning applications in telecom.

telco-bss-engineer.specialisationNote: BSS Engineers work with software systems for business support, making software development skills highly relevant in this field.

telco-capacity-planner.specialisationNote: A Capacity Planner uses data analysis and forecasting skills similar to those of a Data Analyst, focusing on network capacity needs.

telco-cloud-engineer.specialisationNote: A Telco Cloud Engineer uses software development skills, particularly in cloud technologies, making it closely related to the role of a Software Developer.

telco-cloud-platform-architect.specialisationNote: A Cloud & Telco Platform Architect requires software development skills to design and implement cloud solutions, making this career closely related to software development.

telco-compliance-manager.specialisationNote: A Compliance Manager ensures telecom regulations are followed, similar to how a Lawyer interprets and applies laws, making their education paths related.

telco-cpq-architect.specialisationNote: A CPQ Architect designs software systems for pricing and product configuration, which aligns closely with the skills of a Software Developer in creating complex applications.

telco-edge-specialist.specialisationNote: An Edge Computing Specialist designs software solutions for low-latency applications, which is closely related to the work of a Software Developer.

telco-field-engineer.specialisationNote: Field Engineers in telecom often work with electrical systems, similar to Electricians who install and maintain electrical equipment.

telco-fraud-analyst.specialisationNote: A Fraud Analyst uses data analysis skills similar to a Data Analyst, focusing on detecting and preventing fraud in telecom services.

telco-integration-engineer.specialisationNote: Integration Engineers often work with software systems and APIs, making a Software Developer's education relevant for understanding system connections.

telco-it-systems-engineer.specialisationNote: An IT Systems Engineer works closely with software systems, making a Software Developer's education relevant for understanding system operations and applications.
